# Evaluate a machine learning model This sample project has one tutorial to teach you about evaluating a machine learning model using Watson OpenScale. # Tutorials that use this sample project - [Evaluate a machine learning model](https://www.ibm.com/docs/en/cloud-paks/cp-data/5.0.x?topic=ai-evaluate-machine-learning-model) - [Evaluate a deployment in spaces](https://www.ibm.com/docs/en/cloud-paks/cp-data/5.0.x?topic=ai-evaluate-deployment-in-spaces) ## Required services Verify your services: From the navigation menu, choose **Services > Service instances** and look for these services: - **Watson Studio**: For building models. - **Watson Machine Learning**: For saving and deploying the model. - **watsonx.governance**: For monitoring models. ## Assets in this sample project This project contains these sample data assets on the **Assets** page: - The **Data Fabric Trial - Db2 Warehouse** connection to the data for training the model. - The **GoldenBank_HoldOutData.csv** data set for evaluating the model. - The **Mortgage Approval Prediction Model** machine learning model to evaluate. This project also contains one sample notebook. Execute the notebooks step-by-step and provide your credentials when prompted. - **monitor-wml-model-with-watson-OpenScale**: This notebook performs the following functions: - Loads the model deployed in the deployment space. - Programmatically sets up and configure OpenScale. - Creates a data mart for the model with Watson OpenScale and configure OpenScale to monitor that deployment, and inject records and measurements for viewing in the OpenScale Insights dashboard. - Creates a de-biased model if Watson OpenScale reports a bias. - Creates explanations for mortgage approval predictions on sample records. ### Terms and Conditions This project contains Sample Materials, provided under this license.
